item.title: Unchained – Why Pre-IPO Perps Like SpaceX on Hyperliquid Are Seeing an Upswing
item.description:
Pre-IPO trading is hot ahead of three big IPOs. Perp volume on Hyperliquid went from $3M to $44M in three months, and SpaceX perps is just the beginning, says Dio Casares of Patagon.

========================================================

Thank you to our sponsor!


⁠⁠⁠⁠Coinbase One⁠⁠⁠⁠: Get 20% off the first year of your Coinbase One annual plan at ⁠⁠⁠⁠coinbase.com/unchained⁠⁠⁠⁠.

========================================================

Pre-IPO perp volume on Hyperliquid grew from $3 million to $44 million in roughly three months.

Anthropic and OpenAI voided secondary shares, sending shockwaves through the pre-IPO marketes. Robinhood launched trust-style tokenized offerings into a gray area. And three trillion-dollar IPOs — SpaceX, Anthropic, and OpenAI — are converging in the same window.

Dio Casares, founder and CEO of Patagon, a private neobank that has facilitated deals in Anthropic, xAI, Circle, and Kraken, explains the structural difference between derivatives and tokenized spot, why second and third-layer SPV waterfalls are legal hot potato, who actually holds the cleanest title, and where the competition for private market liquidity goes next.


Host:

  • ⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠Laura Shin⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠, Host / Unchained

Guests:

  • ⁠Dio Casares - Founder & CEO, Patagon

Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

]]>

item.title: Once Bitten! A Bitcoin Podcast. – Bitcoin Mining, Block Space As A Commodity And Signalling BIP 110 - Bob Burnett - #610
item.description:
What is it that Bitcoin Miners produce?

$ BTC 77,620

Block height 950,255

Today's guest on the show is Bob Burnett from Barefoot Mining.

He discusses his extensive background in the tech industry, his strong critique of Bitcoin Core development, and his vision for the future of Bitcoin mining, including the importance of BIP-110 and the decentralisation of hash power.

Key Topics:

  • Bob Burnett's background and entry into Bitcoin mining
  • Critique of Bitcoin Core's development process and decision-making
  • BIP-110 (RDTS) soft fork and its temporary role in community reset
  • Distinction between Bitcoin miners and hashers
  • Centralization of hash power and selfish mining attacks
  • Block space as a commodity and the future of derivatives markets
  • Nation-state adoption and the need for sovereign mining
  • Empowering plebs through hash power rental and alternative mining pools (Ocean, Datum)
  • Fungibility of Bitcoin and the value of "virgin Bitcoin"

item.title: The Mustard Seed Bitcoin Podcast – Coffeezilla vs 11%+ Digital Credit Yields and Why Bitcoin is Ready To Run | Joe Consorti
item.description:
Bitcoin is evolving into a unique asset that responds to both crisis and liquidity expansion. In this conversation, we break down how macro policy, market structure, and institutional flows are reshaping bitcoin’s role in global portfolios. The result is a shift toward bitcoin as a core asset for long-term capital allocation.


Timestamps:

0:00 Introduction and current bitcoin market setup

0:23 Price levels, resistance, and defining a new bull market

1:30 Macro backdrop: war, uncertainty, and market reactions

2:40 Bitcoin as chaos insurance across global crises

4:00 Performance vs gold, S&P 500, and shifting perception

5:00 Liquidity asset vs risk-off asset: reconciling the paradox

6:30 Dual drivers: global liquidity and crisis demand

8:00 Declining volatility and shallower bitcoin drawdowns

9:30 Why traditional markets avoid prolonged recessions

12:00 Debt dynamics and the inevitability of policy response

14:00 Faster Fed response cycles from 2008 to today

16:00 The “big print” and bitcoin as the primary beneficiary

17:30 Role of technology and information flow in policy decisions

20:00 Monetary policy, asset inflation, and wealth concentration

21:30 Fastest stock market rally since 1982 and implications

23:30 V-shaped recoveries and forward market returns

25:00 Institutional positioning and bullish equity signals

27:00 Treasury market stress and liquidity risks

28:30 Why markets rise in both good and bad scenarios

30:00 Bitcoin’s origin and alignment with monetary debasement

31:30 Coffeezilla debate and digital credit misconceptions

35:00 STRC mechanics and sustainability of yield

40:00 Bitcoin balance sheet coverage and risk framing

43:30 Bitcoin decoupling from tech and software stocks

46:00 Long-term path toward an uncorrelated asset

46:30 What to own if bitcoin did not exist

48:30 Closing thoughts and where to follow

]]>

item.title: Bitcoin, Fiat & Rock'n'Roll – The Convergence of Capital Markets and Blockchain
item.description:
In this episode of Bitcoin, Fiat & Rock’n’Roll, Stefan Grasmann speaks with Thilo Derenbach, Head of Sales & Business Development Digital Securities Services at Clearstream, about how financial market infrastructure is evolving through digitisation, tokenisation, and blockchain technology. The conversation offers a rare inside perspective from one of Europe’s most important post-trade infrastructure providers and explores how traditional financial institutions are preparing for a hybrid future between legacy and on-chain markets.

Thilo explains the role of Deutsche Börse Group, Clearstream, D7, Crypto Finance, 360X and related initiatives, outlining how the group is building digital-first but hybrid infrastructure for both traditional and blockchain-native market participants. The discussion covers the growing convergence between TradFi and Web3, including Deutsche Börse’s investment into Kraken, partnerships with Ondo and stablecoin issuers, and the broader strategic shift towards interoperable digital capital markets. Stefan and Thilo also discuss recent industry developments such as DTCC’s tokenisation initiative and Bullish acquiring Equiniti for USD 4.2 billion.

A major focus of the episode is collateral mobility and the future reuse of assets across digital markets. Thilo explains why institutional clients increasingly demand more efficient collateral usage, why tokenised securities alone are not enough, and how blockchain technology could fundamentally improve the movement and mobilisation of assets across trading, settlement and custody environments. The episode concludes with a forward-looking discussion on hybrid infrastructure, 24/7 markets, AI-driven financial systems and why the next years may represent a decisive phase in the digitisation of global financial markets.

item.itunes:summary:
In this episode of Bitcoin, Fiat & Rock’n’Roll, Stefan Grasmann speaks with Thilo Derenbach, Head of Sales & Business Development Digital Securities Services at Clearstream, about how financial market infrastructure is evolving through digitisation, tokenisation, and blockchain technology. The conversation offers a rare inside perspective from one of Europe’s most important post-trade infrastructure providers and explores how traditional financial institutions are preparing for a hybrid future between legacy and on-chain markets.

Thilo explains the role of Deutsche Börse Group, Clearstream, D7, Crypto Finance, 360X and related initiatives, outlining how the group is building digital-first but hybrid infrastructure for both traditional and blockchain-native market participants. The discussion covers the growing convergence between TradFi and Web3, including Deutsche Börse’s investment into Kraken, partnerships with Ondo and stablecoin issuers, and the broader strategic shift towards interoperable digital capital markets. Stefan and Thilo also discuss recent industry developments such as DTCC’s tokenisation initiative and Bullish acquiring Equiniti for USD 4.2 billion.

A major focus of the episode is collateral mobility and the future reuse of assets across digital markets. Thilo explains why institutional clients increasingly demand more efficient collateral usage, why tokenised securities alone are not enough, and how blockchain technology could fundamentally improve the movement and mobilisation of assets across trading, settlement and custody environments. The episode concludes with a forward-looking discussion on hybrid infrastructure, 24/7 markets, AI-driven financial systems and why the next years may represent a decisive phase in the digitisation of global financial markets.

item.title: Der Bitcoin Kompass: Wegweiser im Kaninchenbau – Folge 5: Der Bitcoin-Preis – Zwischen Angebot, Nachfrage und Geldpolitik
item.description:
Was bestimmt eigentlich den Bitcoin-Preis? In Folge 5 vom Bitcoin-Kompass sprechen wir üeber eines der meistdiskutierten Themen im Bitcoin-Space: den Preis. Dabei geht es nicht um Prognosen oder Kaufempfehlungen, sondern um ein grundlegendes Verständnis dafüer, wie der Bitcoin-Preis entsteht und welche Kraefte ihn beeinflussen.


Wir erklären, wie Angebot und Nachfrage auf Bitcoin-Börsen funktionieren, warum das Handelsvolumen oft wichtiger ist als der Preis selbst und welche Rolle die Marktkapitalisierung spielt. Ausserdem beleuchten wir die grossen Einflussfaktoren: das Bitcoin-Halving und den Vier-Jahres-Zyklus, die Geldpolitik der US-Notenbank (Fed), Inflation und die wachsenden Staatsschulden der USA. Im Vergleich zwischen Bitcoin und Gold zeigen sie, warum Bitcoin technologisch ueberlegen sein könnte - und warum der Preis letztlich ein Spiegel des wachsenden Vertrauens in diese neue Technologie ist.


Mehr Infos, interaktive Grafiken zur Marktkapitalisierung und Kontaktmoeglichkeiten findet ihr auf:


Webseite: https://derbitcoinkompass.ch

E-Mail: kontakt@derbitcoinkompass.ch

Telegram: https://t.me/btc_kaninchenbau


Feedback und Fragen gerne per E-Mail, ueber Telegram oder ueber unsere Webseite.



(00:00:00) Begruessung & Einfuehrung ins Thema Preis

(00:01:31) Volatilitaet und der aktuelle Bitcoin-Preis

(00:02:33) Kaufkraft, Inflation und warum Menschen investieren

(00:05:20) Marktkapitalisierung: Bitcoin vs. Gold vs. Immobilien vs. Aktien

(00:08:05) Wie entsteht der Bitcoin-Preis? Angebot & Nachfrage erklaert

(00:11:04) Warum das Handelsvolumen entscheidend ist

(00:12:43) Die grossen Einflussfaktoren auf den Bitcoin-Preis

(00:13:26) In was messen wir den Preis? Euro, Dollar oder Aepfel?

(00:17:03) Warum Geld kein stabiler Massstab ist

(00:18:10) Bitcoins festes Angebot: Maximal 21 Millionen

(00:20:02) Das Halving und der Vier-Jahres-Zyklus

(00:24:22) Die Fed, Zinspolitik und US-Staatsschulden

(00:31:27) Was bedeutet das alles fuer den Bitcoin-Preis?

(00:33:37) Bitcoin vs. Gold: Vorteile und Unterschiede

(00:40:30) Preisprognose: Bitcoin auf 1 Million Dollar?

(00:41:54) Zusammenfassung & Verabschiedung

]]>

item.title: This Week in Bitcoin – 105: Yield Panic
item.description:
Bond yields are flashing warning signs, and the policy machine is already looking for ways to keep the Treasury market fed. How the Clarity Act, stablecoins, and the next wave of Bitcoin demand connect. Plus, a quick BIP-110 update and why major miners are pivoting to AI.

Episode Links

  • 🇺🇸 Buy Sats on River - The best way to stack in the US
  • 🇨🇦 The Bitcoin Well - An amazing automatic self-custody Bitcoin platform
  • ⚡ The Bitcoin Company - Spending your Bitcoin Via Lightning
  • 🏦 Fold Card - Pay bills Stack Sats
  • 💵 SALT Lending - Get access to your BTC Value w/out Selling

Show Notes

  • This Bond Selloff Isn't Over Just Yet
  • Arthur Hayes: The Fed will fund the government
  • Sen. Moreno: I paid taxes with Bitcoin in 2016
  • Senate Banking Committee passes the CLARITY Act, 15-9
  • CLARITY Act Clears Senate Banking: What Comes Next?
  • The Facts: The CLARITY Act
  • Warren Statement on No Trump Ethics Provision in Crypto Bill
  • Elizabeth Warren says new crypto legislation will “turbocharge” corruption
  • Lummis-Gillibrand 2023 Bill PDF
  • US Senators Introduce Comprehensive Stablecoin Bill
  • Sen. Cynthia Lummis: Nobody is popping champagne on CLARITY yet
  • Claude recovered Bitcoin worth about $397K
  • 60 Minutes: Insider trading around US military operations
  • 30-year yield clears 5.1% for the first time since 2007
  • How will the Federal Reserve handle rate cuts during an inflation crisis?
  • Bond market inflation fears are rising over oil prices and a new Fed chair
  • Morgan Stanley close to offering crypto trading through E*TRADE
  • Morgan Stanley taps partner to offer crypto to E*TRADE clients
  • David Marcus: China trip shows the surveillance state
  • Fox News host warns of “Big Brother” in China
  • Reuters: US probes suspicious oil trades before Iran pivot
  • Warren, Whitehouse probe suspicious oil trades around Iran announcements
  • US 30-year yield tops 5% as Fed faces inflation test
  • US 30-year bond yield tops 5%
  • Interest rate hikes are less powerful against inflation than conventional wisdom suggests
  • What Is the Relationship Between Inflation and Interest Rates?
  • US Yields at 5% Tug Traders Between Dip-Buying Greed and Fear
  • Citadel’s CEO on the AI boom: “Is it hype? Of course”

Music:

  • Heaven Pool By: Ambriel
]]>

item.title: The Peter McCormack Show – #177 - Rupert Lowe - The State Has Become The Enemy
item.description:
Rupert Lowe joins Peter McCormack to discuss Restore Britain, Reform UK, immigration, Parliament, the judiciary, the civil service, tax, welfare, young people, the Green Party, the private sector, Bitcoin, inflation and whether Britain can still be saved.


Rupert argues that the state has become too powerful, Britain may no longer have the rule of law, the private sector is dying, and Restore’s mission is to return power to the people.


- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - -


TIMESTAMPS:

00:00:00 - The State Has Become the Enemy

00:01:02 - Has Voting Stopped Working?

00:05:49 - How Dysfunctional Is the State?

00:06:58 - The Complaint System Inside Parliament

00:12:46 - “The People Who Lead Britain Are Bad”

00:13:12 - Has Britain Lost the Rule of Law?

00:19:13 - Immigration and the Future of Europe

00:22:59 - What Does Getting Your Country Back Mean?

00:25:39 - Britain’s 22,000-Page Tax Code

00:28:09 - Fabianism and the Growth of the State

00:32:34 - Can One MP Fight the Machine?

00:35:49 - How Restore Mobilised the Apathetic

00:38:48 - Makerfield, Burnham and the By-Election

00:45:39 - Why the Private Sector Is Dying

00:52:17 - How Do You Fix Inflation?

00:59:32 - Is Reform Managed Opposition?

01:08:14 - Restore’s Immigration Policy

01:14:35 - The Triple Lock and the Baby Boomers

item.itunes:summary:
Rupert Lowe joins Peter McCormack to discuss Restore Britain, Reform UK, immigration, Parliament, the judiciary, the civil service, tax, welfare, young people, the Green Party, the private sector, Bitcoin, inflation and whether Britain can still be saved.


Rupert argues that the state has become too powerful, Britain may no longer have the rule of law, the private sector is dying, and Restore’s mission is to return power to the people.

item.title: Bitcoin Fortress – BF216 - Top Bitcoin News - 5/17/26
item.description:
Date: May 17, 2026
Block Height: 949842
Moscow Time: 1280
Fees: 1 / 1 / 2

 

In this episode:

In Episode 216 of The Bitcoin Fortress Podcast, we explore why the real signal this week wasn’t Bitcoin’s price action — it was growing stress inside the legacy financial system itself. We discuss Japan’s bond market turmoil, the advancing CLARITY Act and the fight over developer freedom, why self-custody is increasingly becoming a civil liberties issue, MARA’s massive Bitcoin sale and pivot into AI infrastructure, renewed discussions around privacy in Bitcoin, and what these trends reveal about the future of money, sovereignty, and financial freedom. Plus: Bitcoin podcast universe themes, a featured Bitcoin Fortress Substack post, and a look at the newly released book The Architecture of Money.

 

Bitcoin Fortress YouTube Channel:

https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCEPGN1YCIQcMxkUig5GaNrQ

 

Bitcoin Fortress Substack Featured Post:

Bitcoin vs The Legacy System
https://open.substack.com/pub/bitcoinfortress/p/bitcoin-vs-the-legacy-system

 

Bitcoin Fortress Books:

  • Bitcoin Fortress: A Simple Guide to Bitcoin for Beginners

  • Bitcoin Retirement and Estate Planning

  • The Bitcoin Lifestyle

  • The Architecture of Money

Follow me on Nostr:

npub122fpu8lwu2eu2zfmrymcfed9tfgeray5quj78jm6zavj78phnqdsu3v4h5

 

NEWS (May 10, 2026 – May 17, 2026)

Japan Bond Market / Sovereign Debt Stress
  • Financial Times — Global bond markets rattled as long-term yields surge
    Financial Times – Global Bonds Tumble on Inflation Shock Fears
  • Financial Times — U.S. sells 30-year bonds at 5% yield for first time since 2007

    Trading Economics – Japan 30-Year Bond Yield
  • Financial Times — Japan’s yield panic and global implications
    Financial Times – The Japanese Yield Panic
CLARITY Act / Regulation / Developer Freedom

    Reuters – Senate Committee Advances CLARITY Act

    Reuters – What Is in the Senate Crypto Bill?

    TFTC – Bitcoin Brief Archive
Self-Custody / Privacy / Freedom Tech
  • Bitcoin.org – Bitcoin Is Open Source P2P Money
  • TFTC — Ongoing coverage of privacy, self-custody, and developer issues
    TFTC – Bitcoin Brief Archive
MARA / Mining / AI Infrastructure

item.title: House of Satoshi - Bitcoin und Friends – #58 Live Podcast zum Bitcoin Pizza Day 2026: Abwärtsspirale oder das neue Gold?
item.description:
House of Satoshi und PostFinance feierten den Bitcoin Pizza Day live im KinoKoni Zürich. Mit ausverkauftem Saal, Popcorn in der Hand und grossen Fragen im Raum.


Das sind die Schwerpunkte dieser Episode:
🔥 Panik oder Normalzustand? Was hinter Bitcoins Kursbewegungen wirklich steckt und was Profis sehen, was viele übersehen.
🪙 Digitales Gold - intakte These oder bequeme Illusion? Das Narrativ auf dem Prüfstand: Wie robust ist Bitcoin als Wertspeicher wirklich?
⚛️ Quantum Computing: echte Bedrohung oder viel Lärm um nichts? Wie sicher ist Bitcoin in einer Welt mit neuen Technologien?
🚀 Bitcoin-Dominanz und die Altcoin-Frage: Ist die Hoffnung auf eine Altseason verschwunden oder nur verschoben?
💡 Als Anleger richtig handeln: Wie viel investieren, wie mit Rückschlägen umgehen und welche Strategien sich langfristig bewährt haben.
Mit dabei:

  • Rino Borini - Host House of Satoshi Podcast
  • Alexander Thoma - Head Digital Assets, PostFinance
  • Zana Iseni aka Mrs. Zatoshi - Founder Mrs. Zatoshi & Co-Founder Satoshis Daughters
  • Tim Stingelin - Founder T4 Capital & Bitcoin/Krypto-Entrepreneur

🎧 Viel Substanz, klare Worte, echtes Kino-Feeling. Hör rein!
📬 Noch nicht genug? Hol dir den House of Satoshi Newsletter. Direkt in dein Postfach. Mehr Wissen, mehr Bitcoin, mehr Aha-Momente.

http://www.house-of-satoshi.ch/newsletter


Mehr Infos zum Krypto-Angebot von PostFinance findest du hier https://www.postfinance.ch/de/privat/anlegen/moeglichkeiten/krypto/kryptohandel.html

]]>

item.title: Coin Stories with Natalie Brunell – Michael Saylor & Phong Le Answer Retail Investors' Biggest Questions
item.description:
What's next for Strategy, STRC, and the Bitcoin industry?

In this special live Retail Investor Q&A, Natalie Brunell sits down with Michael Saylor and Phong Le to answer the biggest questions from retail investors.

From dividend changes and mNAV targets to DeFi risk, Bitcoin at $10 million, and whether Strategy plans to sell BTC soon, this conversation covers the issues investors are watching most closely.

Topics include:

  • STRC's proposed move from monthly to semi-monthly dividends
  • Strategy's plan to restore mNAV to 3–4x
  • Risks around DeFi products built on top of STRC
  • What Strategy looks like in a Bitcoin-at-$10-million world
  • Whether Strategy is gearing up to sell Bitcoin

For more information on Strategy's products, visit www.strategy.com/notes

----

Order Natalie's new book "Bitcoin is For Everyone," a simple introduction to Bitcoin and what's broken in our current financial system: https://amzn.to/3WzFzfU

item.title: In Bitcoin We Trust: Ethereum, Blockchain, Cryptocurrency, Fintech Podcast – Bakkt Project Implications : Insitutional Money Flowing In On September 23rd!

In this episode we discuss the potential implications for the cryptocurrency space dealing with the Bakkt Project! What does a sudden influx of institutional money mean for the markets? item.title: State of Bitcoin with Brandon Keys – The European Bitcoin Ban Hiding In Plain Sight with Matyas Kuchar - State of Bitcoin Ep. 238
item.description:
Until last year, more than 1,000 restaurants in Czech Republic accepted Bitcoin. Then it all stopped.

The MiCA regulation didn't ban Bitcoin in Europe. It did something far more effective — it made the rails impossible to operate. And as the Czech merchant economy quietly disappeared, two bigger forces started moving in the background: the petrodollar dying, and CBDCs coming online in Europe THIS YEAR.

In this episode, I sit down with Matyas Kuchar — co-founder of BTC Prague, the largest Bitcoin-only conference in Europe, and one of the most thoughtful voices on European Bitcoin policy — to break down why Czech Republic became the Bitcoin capital of Europe, why the European surveillance state is spreading faster than Americans realize, and why he believes Bitcoin must win because the alternative is just so much worse.

You'll learn:

→ How MiCA quietly killed 1,000+ Bitcoin-accepting restaurants in Czech Republic — and why it wasn't even a direct ban

→ Why Czech Republic became the Bitcoin capital of Europe (Trezor, Slush Pool, BIP-39, General Bytes — all started in Prague)

→ Why the petrodollar is dying — and what the loss of US hegemony means for global decentralization

→ Why Europeans use Bitcoin as money while Americans treat it as digital gold — and which approach is right

→ The European CBDC threat: pilots are launching THIS YEAR — and why most Americans haven't heard about it

→ The CBDC mechanism in Matyas's words: "You can analyze, you can control, you can surveil, you can send from a helicopter"

→ Why even an anarcho-capitalist now meets with politicians — and what changed his mind

→ Why Bitcoin is no longer just savings — it's the network of people building the future you actually want to live in

🚨 JOIN ME AT BTC PRAGUE 2026

The largest Bitcoin-only conference in Europe — June 11-13. Use code GREENCANDLE for discounted tickets:

https://btcprague.com/

🎯 PROTECT YOUR BITCOIN WITH SALT LENDING

Borrow against your Bitcoin without selling a single sat. Green Candle viewers get an exclusive offer:

https://saltlending.com/green-candle

🎯 PLAN YOUR WEALTH WITH STRONG WEALTH

Build a Bitcoin-aligned wealth strategy — estate planning, tax optimization, portfolio construction:

https://strongwealth.net/

🎯 MINE BITCOIN WITH SIMPLE MINING

First-year repairs free for Green Candle listeners:

https://simplemining.io/green-candle

📊 REFERENCED IN THIS EPISODE

• MiCA regulation (EU 2023/1114) — Markets in Crypto-Assets framework

• Czech Republic Bitcoin tax exemption (3-year hold rule)

• Slush Pool (founded 2010 in Prague, first ever Bitcoin mining pool)

• BIP-39 seed phrase standard (developed in Prague)

• Trezor hardware wallet (first ever, founded in Prague)

• EU CBDC pilot programs (launching 2026)

• BTC Prague 2026 conference — June 11-13

Bitcoin #BTCPrague #Matyas #MiCA #BitcoinEurope #CBDC #BitcoinPolicy #EuropeanBitcoin #BitcoinConference #SoundMoney #Petrodollar #BitcoinAdoption #CzechRepublic #FinancialFreedom #BitcoinNews

🔔 SUBSCRIBE for weekly Bitcoin, macro, and personal finance content.

⚠️ Disclaimer: This video is for informational purposes only. Not financial advice. Always do your own research.

]]>

item.title: Bitcoiners - Live From Bitcoin Beach – Uncle Rockstar Dev: BTCPay Server, Cypherpunk Ethos, Bitcoin Circular Economies and El Salvador
item.description:
Is the Wall Street ETF narrative killing peer-to-peer Bitcoin adoption? Is Bitcoin failing if it only becomes a corporate store of value hoarded on Wall Street? 

Institutional demand and exchange-traded funds are not the end game for hyperbitcoinization. True freedom requires building an alternative economic system entirely outside legacy banking, proving that Bitcoin must function as everyday money to succeed.

Uncle Rockstar Dev (@r0ckstardev) unpacks how open source software protects financial sovereignty. The cypherpunk history of BTCPay Server demonstrates how a non-custodial payment gateway allows anyone to host a node without asking permission. Relying on a centralized crypto payment processor intermediates your wealth, meaning you must self-host your infrastructure to enforce individual sovereignty.

A thriving circular economy operates directly on the ground. From kids using the Lightning Network to buy choco bananas in El Zonte to alternative networks expanding across Africa and Indonesia, communities are establishing localized ecosystems. These regions completely bypass legacy structures, choosing instead to settle daily medium of exchange transactions directly in Satoshis.

Documenting this global shift requires a dedicated grassroots movement of creators who reject mainstream financial media. Independent documentarians Zack Dorsey (@zackdorseyx)  and Brandon Martin (@elbrandonmartin) share their proof of work traveling from Central America to Mauritius to capture peer to peer adoption. Capturing these alternative networks on camera is vital to countering corporate narratives and demonstrating how local financial inclusion scales from the bottom up.

This decentralized evolution dismantles the broken, top-down corporate NGO model. Instead of creating loops of financial dependency, the leaders meeting at the Zonte Economic Forum are connecting their localized circles to build global network synergy. If you are ready to stop accumulating fiat and start participating in the parallel economy, smash that subscribe button, leave your thoughts on economic sovereignty below, and share this with someone still paying with dirty cash.

—Bitcoin Beach Team



Connect and Learn more about the guests:
Uncle Rockstar (X): https://x.com/r0ckstardev
Jethro Toro (X): https://x.com/JethroToro
Brandon Martin (X): https://x.com/elbrandonmartin
Zack Dorsey: (X):  https://x.com/zackdorseyx


Support and follow Bitcoin Beach:
X: https://www.twitter.com/BitcoinBeach 
IG: https://www.instagram.com/bitcoinbeach_sv 
TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@livefrombitcoinbeach 
Web: https://www.bitcoinbeach.com 


Browse through this quick guide to learn more about the episode:
00:00  Intro
02:21  How to accept Bitcoin for business using self hosted BTCPay Server
04:16  BTCPay Server vs BitPay: Why Nicolas Dorier built an open source alternative
06:30  Why hyperbitcoinization depends entirely on grassroots adoption
10:02  What a real Bitcoin standard looks like in El Salvador and globally
11:42  Will Bitcoin fail if it only becomes a Wall Street store of value
12:42  Why Bitcoin Beach rejected the centralized fiat NGO funding model
21:23  Proof of Work journalism: Independent media reporting on El Salvador
24:44  What it is really like moving to El Salvador to live on Bitcoin
27:31  How connecting peer to peer networks creates global monetary synergy




Live From Bitcoin Beach

]]>

item.title: Münzcrime - True Crime Bitcoin Podcast – Mt. Gox: "Withdrawal denied". Episode 5
item.description:
Doch der Weg ins Chaos war kein einziger Sprung. Es begann mit einer Serie von Sicherheitspannen und technischem Versagen – Warnsignale, die ignoriert wurden. Diese Inkompetenz schuf ein Vakuum, ein digitales Schlupfloch, durch das schließlich nicht nur Fehler, sondern echte Angreifer strömten. Erst die chronischen Pannen, dann der massive Hack, der das System endgültig zum Einsturz brachte.

Willkommen zur fünften Ausgabe unseres Podcasts. Wir sind tief in den Fall Mt. Gox eingetaucht, um diesen komplexen Skandal chronologisch zu entwirren. Von den bescheidenen Anfängen in der Frühzeit von Bitcoin bis hin zu den heutigen, kaum zu fassenden Auswüchsen dieses historischen Desasters. Wir zeigen euch, wie aus einer kleinen Nischenplattform einer der größten Bitcoin-Skandale aller Zeiten wurde – und warum die Folgen bis heute nachhallen.

V4V:
crime21@getalby.com

item.title: The Bitcoin Infinity Show – Bitcoin Core Has Been Compromised | Matthew Kratter | BIS #203
item.description:
Matthew Kratter joins the Bitcoin Infinity Show to talk about the escalating fight against spam on Bitcoin, why he believes Bitcoin Core has been compromised, and how the pleb migration to Bitcoin Knots has gone from 1% to 25% of the network in under two years. Kratter walks through the case for BIP-110 as a temporary soft fork, explains why mining pool centralization is the structural problem underneath everything else, and shares his own setup for renting hashrate at industrial scale from home using Datum Gateway and Ocean. The conversation also covers the rotting culture inside Bitcoin Core, why slaying our heroes still matters when figures like Adam Back behave erratically, and what self-custody, sound money, and Bitcoin sovereignty actually demand from us when the protocol itself is under cultural attack.

Connect with Matthew:

https://x.com/mattkratter

https://www.bitcoinuniversity.com/

https://www.bitcoinuniversity.com/mining

Connect with Us:

https://www.bitcoininfinityshow.com/

https://bitcoininfinitystore.com

https://primal.net/infinity

https://primal.net/knut

https://primal.net/luke

https://twitter.com/BtcInfinityShow

https://twitter.com/knutsvanholm

https://twitter.com/lukedewolf

Thanks to our sponsors - check out their websites for info:

Firefish: https://firefish.io/ - Use Code INFINITY!

Bull Bitcoin: https://app.bullbitcoin.com/registration/infinity

BitBox: https://bitbox.swiss/infinity - Use Code INFINITY for 5% off!

Bitcoin Adviser: https://content.thebitcoinadviser.com/freedom

The Bitcoin Infinity Show is a Bitcoin podcast hosted by Knut Svanholm and produced by Luke de Wolf.

]]>

item.title: Bitcoin Archive w Archie – Astrophysicist Destroys Michael Saylor's Bitcoin Model
item.description:
In this conversation with Stephen Perrenod, astrophysicist, quantum computing expert, and co-founder of the Scientific Bitcoin Institute. we explore Bitcoin through the lens of physics, network theory, and power law dynamics.

We also discuss Bitcoin’s relationship to gold, the limits of exponential growth, the risks facing Bitcoin treasury companies, and whether quantum computing poses a serious threat to the network.

⚠️ Bitcoin may not be following a speculative cycle at all, but a deeply embedded network-driven power law that reflects how complex systems grow over time.


CONNECT WITH STEPHEN PERRENOD

X: ⁠https://x.com/moneyordebt⁠

Substack: ⁠https://substack.com/@stephenperrenod⁠


FOLLOW THE PODCAST

📺 YOUTUBE - ⁠https://bit.ly/BitcoinArchiveY⁠ 

⚡️ RUMBLE - ⁠https://bit.ly/BitcoinArchiveRU⁠ 

🌳 AMAZON - ⁠https://bit.ly/BitcoinArchiveAM⁠


CONNECT WITH ARCHIE

X/Twitter: ⁠https://bit.ly/BitcoinArchiveX

item.title: Store of Bitcoin - Bitcoin as Store of Value – Ep. 49 - Daniel Batten | A new different wave of Bitcoin Adopters.
item.description:
In this episode of Store of Bitcoin, host Alessandro Ottaviani welcomes Daniel Batten, a prominent Bitcoin analyst and investor, to discuss the evolving narrative surrounding Bitcoin, particularly in relation to energy consumption and environmental impact. Daniel shares insights on how the mainstream media's perception of Bitcoin has shifted from skepticism to a more favorable view, largely due to rigorous research and advocacy from the Bitcoin community. He emphasizes the importance of effectively communicating Bitcoin's benefits to various stakeholders, including policymakers and investors, to foster broader adoption.

The conversation delves into the challenges of reaching new adopters, particularly those who may not be motivated by the same values that initially attracted early Bitcoin enthusiasts. Daniel highlights the need for a more nuanced approach to education and outreach, focusing on the social and environmental benefits of Bitcoin. He also discusses the potential for Bitcoin to serve as a tool for financial inclusion in developing countries, where it can provide a safeguard against inflation and empower individuals in oppressive regimes. The episode concludes with a look at future trends in Bitcoin adoption and the importance of aligning messaging with the values of diverse audiences.

This episode is sponsored by ⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠BitVault⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠, the new open-source & self-custody wallet utilizing time-delays and multisignature to offset physical attacks and hacks.


Follow us on:

Website: ⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠https://storeofbitcoin.com/⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠

X.com: ⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠https://twitter.com/StoreofBitcoin⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠

YouTube chanel:⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠ https://www.youtube.com/@StoreofBitcoin00⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠

He also discusses the potential for Bitcoin to serve as a tool for financial inclusion in developing countries, where it can provide a safeguard against inflation and empower individuals in oppressive regimes. The episode concludes with a look at future trends in Bitcoin adoption and the importance of aligning messaging with the values of diverse audiences.

This episode is sponsored by ⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠BitVault⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠, the new open-source & self-custody wallet utilizing time-delays and multisignature to offset physical attacks and hacks.

item.title: Raoul Pal: The Journey Man – The Global Economy Is Changing Rapidly
item.description:
Raoul Pal and GMI's head of macro research Julien Bittel, CFA, open their biweekly "Shooting the Shit" episode, which is normally exclusive to Real Vision Alpha members and above, to everyone. It's a sneak peek into how the guys brainstorm, interpret charts and look for opportunities through the macro lens. In this episode, they break down the forces driving markets right now, from global liquidity and crypto regulation to AI, compute, energy, stablecoins, and they explain why the old business cycle framework may be losing power as the exponential age accelerates.


Today’s sponsor is Plus500 US. Take your trading to the next level with cross-market contracts, from precious metals to key indices, and more. Whether you’re a seasoned trader in the Futures arena or brand new, Plus500’s user-friendly trading platform offers you the advanced tools, market insights, and quick execution you’ve been looking for.
 Get started with Plus500 for as little as $100 at https://us.plus500.com. Trading in futures involves the risk of loss.

item.title: Rob Wallace | Bitcoin News – Nobody's Ready For What The Clarity Act Just Did To Bitcoin | Joe Consorti
item.description:
The Clarity Act just changed everything for Bitcoin — and almost no one is talking about it.

Joe Consorti joins the show for a deep dive into why the Clarity Act could be the single biggest regulatory unlock in Bitcoin's history, and the chain of events it's about to set off on a global scale.

Here's what we cover:

• Why the Clarity Act could trigger a sovereign-level Bitcoin accumulation race — and which countries move first

• The Strait of Hormuz crisis: how a full closure could send oil past $200 a barrel — and why Wall Street is completely asleep at the wheel

• The inflationary time bomb quietly building beneath the surface of markets

• AI and the biggest productivity boom in history — why governments will be forced to print in response and how Bitcoin becomes the ultimate beneficiary

If you think Bitcoin's regulatory moment is already priced in, this conversation will change your mind.

]]>

item.title: einemillionsatoshi - der Bitcoin Podcast – Bitcoin und Beziehung: Warum Überzeugen selten funktioniert
item.description:
Deine Begeisterung für Bitcoin ist riesig, aber zu Hause stößt du auf Granit? In dieser Folge von "einemillionsatoshi" erfährst du, wie du das Thema Bitcoin in deine Beziehung bringst, ohne einen Familienstreit auszulösen. Ich bespreche, warum technisches Fachchinesisch oft der falsche Weg ist und wie du stattdessen über gesellschaftliche Werte und echte Lösungen für Alltagsprobleme wie die Inflation sprichst.

item.title: Bitcoin Magazine Podcast – How China Hijacked Bernie & AOC's War on AI | Bitcoin Policy Hour EP 38
item.description:
On April 29th, the US Senate hosted a panel on the "existential threat" of AI and two of the four panelists worked for the Chinese government. One month earlier, Bernie Sanders and AOC introduced legislation imposing a federal moratorium on American AI data centers. On Bitcoin Policy Hour EP 38, Zack Cohen, Ken Egan, and Zack Shapiro unpack a new Bitcoin Policy Institute report by Sam Lyman exposing the CCP influence operation steering US AI policy. They also cover the Clarity Act vote in Senate Banking, the BRCA fight, and the Digital Asset Parity Act.



Sam Lyman's BPI Report: https://www.btcpolicy.org/articles/foreign-influence-in-the-campaign-against-american-ai

🔶 Connect with Zack Shapiro on X: https://x.com/zackbshapiro🔶 Connect with Zack Cohen on X: https://x.com/zackcohen_🔶 Connect with Ken Egan on X: https://x.com/Bayman11771🔶 Learn more about the Bitcoin Policy Institute: https://www.btcpolicy.org/


CHAPTERS

00:00 — Cold Open: The Game Theory of AGI and Why It's Already Inevitable

00:37 — Inside the Senate Panel That Platformed CCP Officials on US AI Policy

06:43 — Zack Shapiro on Why This Is a Foreign Influence Operation, Not a Debate

12:12 — Campus Backlash, NIMBYism, and the Misinformation Fueling Anti-AI Sentiment

22:22 — Recursive Self-Improvement, AI Doom Risk, and the Limits of Regulation

28:15 — US vs China: Closed Frontier Models vs Open Source and the Compute Race

35:32 — Power Is the Real Bottleneck: Gas Turbines, SMRs, and the Dyson Swarm Endgame

42:32 — Infinite Demand for Inference, the AI Bubble, and the Coming IPO Wave

48:39 — Clarity Act Passes Senate Banking 15-9: What Survives the Floor Vote

51:28 — BRCA Under Threat, Digital Asset Parity Act, and Final Call to Action



#Bitcoin #BitcoinPolicyHour #BitcoinPolicyInstitute #BPI #BernieSanders #AOC #AI #ArtificialIntelligence #China #CCP #USChinaCompetition #DataCenters #AIPolicy #ClarityAct #BRCA #DigitalAssetParityAct #BitcoinMining #SenateBanking #ZackShapiro #KenEgan #SamLyman #AGI #AISafety #EnergyPolicy #NuclearPower #SpaceX #AIBubble #FrontierModels #Anthropic #BitcoinMagazine #BitcoinConference


DISCLAIMER: The views and opinions expressed in this show are those of the participants and do not necessarily reflect the official policy or position of BTC Inc., Bitcoin Magazine, or any affiliated entities. This content is provided for informational and educational purposes only and should not be construed as investment, legal, tax, or accounting advice. Nothing contained in this show constitutes a solicitation, recommendation, endorsement, or offer to buy or sell any securities or financial instruments. Viewers should consult their own advisors before making financial or business decisions.

]]>

item.title: Crypto Nerds Podcast - Krypto, Bitcoin, Web3 & Co. – Uncut | Flash Crash, MiCA 2.0 und FED-Direktzugang für Krypto
item.description:
Diese Episode wird wie immer unterstützt von unserem Partner und Krypto-Broker Bison https://bit.ly/cryptonerds-bison 10 Euro BISON Gutschein (Code: BITCOIN10): https://bisonapp.com/gutschein/podcast

Jetzt Early Access sichern - Crypto Nerds Trading Lab Lerne Trading, teste Strategien mit Demogeld und nimm an Wettbewerben teil, komplett risikofrei. Jetzt kostenlos registrieren: https://tradinglab.cryptonerds.com/

Einordnung & Überblick Eine turbulente Woche für Krypto, aber die Ursachen liegen größtenteils außerhalb des Marktes. Eric und Sascha schauen sich den Flash Crash, den EU-Regulierungsrahmen, den FED-Direktzugang und die Lage auf dem Perpetual-Trading-Markt genau an.

Themen dieser Woche

• Vienna Blockchain Week: Wien siedelt Bybit, KuCoin und weitere Börsen unter MiCA an

• CASP-Lizenzen: Deadline 1. Juli 2026, wer jetzt noch nicht dran ist, ist zu spät

• Bitcoin Flash Crash unter 76.000 Dollar: Long-Liquidationen und Kaskadeneffekt erklärt

• Makro-Ursachen: US-Inflation, Treasury-Renditen über 5,1 Prozent, Iran-Pattsituation

item.title: Bitcoin News Alerts | Daily BTC Macro Signal – 2347: 1 Million Bitcoin Arms Race Begins - Congress Triggers Supply War
item.description:
Congress has introduced legislation targeting a Strategic Bitcoin Reserve of up to 1,000,000 BTC - raising a much bigger question: what happens when sovereign buyers begin competing for a finite supply? With Jack Mallers calling Bitcoin a play on a $400-$500 trillion global savings market and Michael Saylor arguing America should acquire up to 25% of total supply, today's episode breaks down why Bitcoin's repricing may be entering a new phase.

📺 Prefer video? Watch daily Bitcoin breakdowns: http://BitcoinNewsAlerts.net

]]>

item.title: BITCOIN & ALTCOINS – Korrekturen sind positive Normalität
item.description:
In den vergangenen Wochen deuteten viele Zeichen auf eine Erholung und den Beginn einer Aufwärtsbewegung beim Bitcoin hin. Doch die Geduld der Marktteilnehmer wird erneut auf die Probe gestellt: Ende letzter Woche fiel die Krypto-Leitwährung unter die viel beachtete Marke von 80.000 Dollar und notiert weiterhin darunter. Was hinter diesem Rücksetzer steckt, ob es sich um einen nachhaltigen Bruch handelt, wie man sich jetzt positionieren sollte und was Anlegern in naher Zukunft bei Bitcoin und Altcoins bevorsteht, erläutert Oliver Michel (tokentus investment AG) in einer neuen Folge von „BITCOIN & ALTCOINS“.

Hinweis: Die im Podcast besprochenen Coins und Token stellen keine spezifischen Kauf- oder Anlageempfehlungen dar. Die Moderatoren oder der Verlag haften nicht für etwaige Verluste, die aufgrund der Umsetzung der im Podcast diskutierten Gedanken oder Ideen entstehen.

Das Gespräch wurde am 11.05.2026 an der Frankfurter Börse aufgezeichnet.

Tägliche Informationen gefällig? Dann ab zu „Krypto-Insights“ – dem Telegram-Kanal von Oliver Michel und Johanna Krämer. Weitere Infos unter www.krypto-insights.de

]]>

item.title: All In Bitcoin – 032 American Hodl: Bitcoin is Forever War | KNOTS vs Core & BlackRock | All in Bitcoin w/CK
item.description:
🎙️American Hodl: Bitcoin is Forever War | KNOTS vs Core & BlackRock | All in Bitcoin w/CK

American HODL got into Bitcoin in 2014. He's seen every cycle since, every war, every wave of new people arrive and dilute the culture he helped build. In this episode he doesn't hold back.

Nietzsche, René Girard, and whether Bitcoiners become the thing they fight. The block size war and the PTSD it left behind. Why the KNOTS vs Core debate is a sideshow — and what BlackRock is quietly doing to Bitcoin's development culture that actually matters. The four-year cycle: broken or evolving? And the big unresolved tension that will define Bitcoin's future: freedom versus number go up.

Plus: when is deception moral? What Bukele got right. Why Europe is headed for civil war. And why Bitcoin is not a phase, not a moment — it's forever war.

And we're making a Bitcoin movie: BIP-110, KNOTS vs Core. You'll have to watch to get the pitch!

Bitcoin #AmericanHODL #BitcoinPodcast #BitcoinMaximalism #BlockSizeWar #BlackRock #BitcoinForeverWar #Cypherpunks #btc

https://linktr.ee/allinbitcoin

Nostr: hodl@primal.net

👇FOLLOW CK & ALL IN BITCOIN:

Nostr: allinbitcoin@primal.net

allinbitcoin@iris.to

allinbitcoin@nostrplebs.com

X: @allinbitcoinpod

2:06 Pseudonymity, identity, and inner truth

4:26 Nietzsche, Girard, and avoiding becoming a monster

6:03 Institutional betrayal led to Bitcoin

14:32 British vs American humor: HODL responds

19:18 Raising children with agency and responsibility

item.title: THE Bitcoin Podcast – Bitcoin Kidnappings, AI Slop, Quantum FUD, and Memetic Warfare | coinjoined Chris
item.description:
In this episode of THE Bitcoin Podcast, Walker sits down with coinjoined Chris for a wide-ranging conversation covering Bitcoin security, privacy, memes, AI propaganda, self-custody, and the future of freedom tech. They discuss the rise in violent “$5 wrench attacks” against Bitcoiners, operational security best practices, and why privacy tools and self-custody matter more than ever in an increasingly surveilled world.

They also dive deep into Bitcoin scaling debates, covenant proposals, quantum computing fears, and whether Bitcoin should continue evolving or ossify over time. Along the way, they explore AI-generated media, the changing culture of Bitcoin, the rise of institutional “Suitcoiners,” and why memes remain one of the most powerful forms of modern propaganda.

The conversation closes with reflections on sovereignty, CBDCs, the future of money, and a philosophical speed round on plebs, voting, podcasts, and the fall of Rome.

FOLLOW CHRIS:

X: https://x.com/coinjoined

item.title: Bitcoin.com News Weekly Update – Bonds Crack at 5%, Saylor’s Bitcoin Pivot, Hyperliquid vs CME & CLARITY Drama
item.description: <![CDATA[This week, David Sencil and Graham Stone unpack a heavy macro week — from the 30-year US Treasury clearing 5% for the first time since 2007, to Strategy’s $1.5B convertible note buyback and Michael Saylor quietly testing the market on Bitcoin sales.They dig into Iran’s reported “Hormuz Safe” Bitcoin sanctions-bypass scheme, why David thinks Bitcoin is actually worse than fiat for evading sanctions, and the Coinbase/Circle/Hyperliquid USDC deal that triggered CME and ICE to run to regulators.Plus: David’s CFTC Chairman Michael Selig interview from Vegas, Austin Campbell on the Clarity Act, and Graham vs David on whether the bill actually passes by July 4th.Topics covered:• 30-year US Treasury clears 5% — what it means for hard assets• Iran’s Hormuz Safe Bitcoin maritime insurance play• Why David thinks Bitcoin is worse than fiat for sanctions evasion• Strategy’s $1.5B convertible note buyback and Saylor’s pivot• STRC (“stretch”) — the 11.5% Bitcoin yield play• Hyperliquid lands USDC deal with Coinbase and Circle• CME and ICE lobbying regulators over Hyperliquid• David’s interview with CFTC Chairman Michael Selig• Will the CLARITY Act pass by July 4th? Graham and David debate00:22 - Welcome and Guest Introduction00:58 - Bitcoin and Market Overview02:26 - Bond Market Analysis04:21 - Bitcoin's Relevance to Bonds07:34 - Japanese Bond Market Crisis08:25 - Iran's Bitcoin Maritime Insurance12:36 - Bitcoin and Privacy Coins14:07 - Strategy's Bitcoin Sales Strategy18:43 - Interview with Michael Saylor22:12 - Hyperliquid and USDC Deal25:58 - Regulatory Challenges for Hyperliquid32:03 - Clarity Act and Political Implications39:33 - Closing Remarks]]>

item.title: The Why Bitcoin Show – #100 - 100 Not Out: Reflections, Regrets and Why I'm Just Getting Started
item.description:
Episode 100. No intro. No ads. Just me.

This one is different. Just an honest reflection on 100 episodes, what this journey has actually been like, and where things are heading.

I share what drove me to start the show, the momentum I built and lost, the guests who genuinely changed how I think, and something personal I haven't said publicly before about confidence, doubt, and what it's taken to finally back myself.

I also explain why I built My Bitcoin Will, a DIY Bitcoin inheritance starter kit designed to help people get off zero and start closing the gap between law, access, and education.

If you're not ready to dive in yet, start with the free Bitcoin Inheritance Stress Test or download the checklist at My Bitcoin Will.

Also, some gentle shilling from my side. I've written a book. No Tenants, No Troubles: The Case for Digital Over Real Estate. Available on Amazon now: https://www.dalewarburton.com/book

Need a hand with self-custody? Book a session with me to get your Bitcoin security house in order.

And get on Nostr if you haven't already. It's a game changer. npub1r94hh3kt6re3j5jv7yg8rw0ah5fg90vw9pjklxzknvhdkx0famfsn0njzt

]]>

item.title: Bitcoin Audible – Chat_168 - Hashing for Heat with Tyler Stevens
item.description:
"Our little home controller, building controller, it knows our electric rate, it knows the way that electric rate changes based on the time of day. It knows the PV flux, the solar flux from the solar panels. So it knows if there's excess power that the building's not using.
It also knows our natural gas rate. It also knows the Bitcoin price and the network difficulty. This is all just in our software.
And in real time, our building can choose, it can flip between heating with gas or with hashrate to save you the most money."

~ Tyler Stevens

I am constantly shocked by how much of our everyday infrastructure is basically begging for a Bitcoin upgrade. Almost half of the world's energy is used for heat, and a quarter of it is just "comfort heat" to keep our homes and water warm. What if we generated all of that with bitcoin miners instead?

I sat down with Tyler Stevens, a former aerospace thermal engineer who is currently helping spearhead the Hashrate Heatpunks movement. I’ve been running my own hacky miner-heater setup downstairs to survive the winter, but Tyler and the guys at the 256 Foundation are taking this to an entirely different scale. We talked about stripping away our reliance on monopolistic giants like Bitmain and finally building a fully open-source mining stack.

The stakes here are high. If even a fraction of normal households swap their standard electric heating elements for ASIC chips, we're looking at a huge decentralization shift for the grid and an explosion in Bitcoin's security budget. We also get into the reality of sovereign smart homes, avoiding creepy cloud-connected thermostats, and why the "energy waste" critics are entirely blind to how thermodynamics actually works. This was an incredibly fun conversation and it proves we are still incredibly early to what home mining will eventually look like.

Guest Links

  • Tyler on X (Link: https://twitter.com/tylerkstevens)
  • Exergy's Website (Link: https://exergyheat.com/)
  • 256 Foundation's Website (Link: https://www.256foundation.org/)
  • Hashrate Heatpunks' Website (Link: https://heatpunks.org/)
  • The Space's Website (Link: https://denver.space/)

Affiliate Links

  • Become sovereign, hold your keys, be censorship resistant with the Bitbox hardware wallet. Get 5% off everything in the store with code GUY (Link: https://bitbox.swiss/)
  • Get ⁠10% off the COLDCARD⁠ with code BITCOINAUDIBLE ⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠(Link: https://bitcoinaudible.com/coldcard)
  • Get 10% off the best Bitcoin board game in the world, HODLUP! Or any of the other great games from The Free Market Kids! Use code GUY10 at checkout for 10% off your cart! (Link: https://www.freemarketkids.com/collections/games-1)
  • (Under construction) Check out the list of products and services I use and recommend on BitcoinAudible.com (Link: https://bitcoinaudible.com/)

Host Links

  • ⁠Guy on Nostr ⁠(Link: http://tinyurl.com/2xc96ney)
  • ⁠Guy on X ⁠(Link: https://twitter.com/theguyswann)
  • Guy on Instagram (Link: https://www.instagram.com/theguyswann)

]]>

item.title: BitBucks Bitcoin Podcast – Bitcoin und Deutschland: Wo stehen wir und wo kann die Reise hingehen? #6 mit Larry
item.description:
Herzlich willkommen zum BitBucks Bitcoin Podcast(https://www.bitbucks.de)!

Heute haben wir einen besonderen Gast: Larry, bekannt als @B1tcoin3r auf X. Larry ist ein erfahrener Bitcoin-Experte, Vortragsredner und Workshop-Host. Dank seines weitreichenden Netzwerks in führenden Positionen bringt er tiefgehende Einblicke in die aktuelle Bitcoin-Situation in Deutschland mit. Gemeinsam sprechen wir über die deutsche Bitcoin-Politik, die Auswirkungen der neuen Regierung und die mögliche Zukunft von Bitcoin in Deutschland.

Viel Spaß beim Zuhören!

Zudem reden wir auch noch über die Themen:

- Die aktuelle Bitcoin Lage in Deutschland (Adaption, Beliebtheit)

- Lösungen für eine Bitcoin Adaption

- Einfluss der Politik auf Bitcoin

- Ob Bitcoin die Politik überhaupt braucht- Bitcoin und Unternehmen bzw. Start-Ups

- Wie viele Jahre es dauern wird, bis Deutschland Bitcoin als offizielles Zahlungsmittel einführt

- Bitcoin Städte Spiel: Errate die fünf beliebtesten Bitcoin Städte weltweit

- Wie Bitcoin Larrys Leben verändert hat

- Glaskugel: Bitcoin Preis am Ende des Jahres 2025Podcast Folge auf Spotify:

---------------------------------------------------------------------

https://www.bitbucks.de/Apple Download: https://apps.apple.com/de/app/bitcoin-wallet-bitbucks/id1453167599

Google Play: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=de.fuf.bitbucks&pli=1

https://www.instagram.com/bit_bucks/

https://www.linkedin.com/company/bitbucks/

https://www.tiktok.com/@bitbucks

https://x.com/bit_bucksPodcast auf Spotify:

https://open.spotify.com/show/0bhDawE23ld6lPw7KnqUH5?si=5182b2525e7144db

----------------------------------------------------------------------

Vernetz dich gerne mit den Rednern :)

LinkedIn Konsti Schwemlein: https://www.linkedin.com/in/konstantin-schwemlein/

X/Twitter Larry: https://x.com/B1tcoin3r

--------------------------------------------------------------

TimeStamps:

00:00 Intro

07:45 Thema Bitcoin: Bevölkerung vs. Unternehmen/Politik

Viel Spaß beim Zuhören!

Zudem reden wir auch noch über die Themen:

- Die aktuelle Bitcoin Lage in Deutschland (Adaption, Beliebtheit)

- Lösungen für eine Bitcoin Adaption

item.title: Surfin' Bitcoin – Il met du BITCOIN dans la trésorerie de sa PME de 100 salariés (retour terrain)
item.description:
Comment un dirigeant de PME en place une partie de la trésorerie de son entreprise en Bitcoin ? Pourquoi ? Combien ? Avec quelles précautions ?


Dans cet épisode, Jonathan Herscovici reçoit Wojtek Jamka, cofondateur de Web-Atrio (https://www.web-atrio.com), une ESN française de 100 salariés et 9 M€ de chiffre d'affaires, cliente de StackinSat en DCA automatique.


Wojtek partage sans filtre son retour d'expérience de dirigeant qui a fait le choix d'allouer une partie de la trésorerie excédentaire de sa société en Bitcoin.


Au programme de cet épisode :

➤ Pourquoi les comptes à terme bancaires sont structurellement perdants face à l'inflation

➤ Comment son histoire personnelle (né dans la Pologne soviétique) a forgé sa compréhension de la dévaluation monétaire

➤ La méthodologie concrète de Web-Atrio : DCA mensuel + acceptation des paiements clients en Bitcoin

➤ La règle des 5 à 10 % de trésorerie blocable à ne pas dépasser

➤ Self custody, multisignature, protocoles anti-enlèvement : sécuriser les Bitcoin d'une entreprise

➤ Bitcoin direct, ETF, Bitcoin Treasury Companies : quel véhicule choisir selon son profil

➤ Comment convaincre un dirigeant qui hésite


Un épisode complémentaire de notre interview avec Alexandre Laizet (Capital B) sur les Bitcoin Treasury Companies, cette fois côté PME non cotée.


📊 Vous êtes dirigeant et souhaitez placer une partie de votre trésorerie en Bitcoin ?

StackinSat accompagne les entreprises dans la gestion de leur trésorerie BTC.

👉 https://stackinsat.com/signup/1?r=SURFIN


00:00 – Introduction : Wojtek Jamka, dirigeant de Web-Atrio (ESN, 100 salariés)

03:12 – Pourquoi Bitcoin pour une PME ? Comptes à terme, inflation et trésorerie qui se déprécie

10:58 – De la Pologne soviétique à Bitcoin : ce qu'on apprend quand sa monnaie ne vaut plus rien

15:38 – Conviction personnelle ou décision rationnelle ? La double lecture du dirigeant

17:40 – DCA, paiements clients en BTC : combien Web-Atrio alloue réellement, et la limite des 5 à 10 %

23:27 – Volatilité et sommeil du dirigeant : pourquoi le DCA bat le trading

28:05 – Self custody, multisig, protection contre l'enlèvement : sécuriser les BTC d'une entreprise

30:56 – Quel profil de PME peut franchir le pas ? Frenchtech, industrie, artisans

35:18 – Bitcoin direct, ETF, Bitcoin Treasury Companies : quel véhicule choisir ?

38:19 – Décision financière ou philosophique ? Et comment convaincre un dirigeant qui hésite

43:48 – Conclusion


🎓 Pour comprendre Bitcoin avant d'investir, suivez notre formation "Investir en Bitcoin" :

👉 https://www.investir-en-bitcoin.com/


📉 Mesurez la perte de pouvoir d'achat de votre trésorerie avec notre simulateur d'inflation :

👉 https://stackinsat.com/simulateur-inflation



🔔 Abonnez-vous à Surfin' Bitcoin pour ne manquer aucun épisode

👍 Likez la vidéo si elle vous a plu

💬 Posez vos questions en commentaires



⚠️ Cette vidéo ne constitue pas un conseil en investissement. Bitcoin est un actif volatil. N'investissez que ce que vous êtes prêt à perdre. Faites vos propres recherches.

Hébergé par Acast. Visitez acast.com/privacy pour plus d'informations.

]]>

item.title: Bald in Bitcoin – Bald in Bitcoin - Folge 13: Die stille Steuer – Warum der Staat Inflation nicht bekämpfen will
item.description:
Deine Eltern haben dir gesagt: Leg Geld zurück. Sparen lohnt sich.

Guter Rat – damals. Aber heute ist etwas kaputt.

In Folge 12 haben wir gezeigt, warum dein Kontostand stabil aussehen kann und du trotzdem ärmer wirst. Heute gehen wir einen Schritt weiter und stellen die unbequeme Frage dahinter: Warum wird Inflation nicht einfach abgestellt?

Die Antwort: Weil das System genau so gebaut ist.

DAS LERNST DU IN DIESER FOLGE

– Warum Staaten gigantische Schulden durch Inflation heimlich entwerten – Was finanzielle Repression bedeutet – und warum das kein Verschwörungsmythos ist – Wer wirklich die Rechnung zahlt: die stille Steuer auf deine Ersparnisse – Warum das 2%-Ziel der EZB keine zufällige Zahl ist – Was Bitcoin als mengenmäßig begrenztes System damit zu tun hat

WEITERFÜHRENDE EMPFEHLUNGEN

Der Bitcoin-Standard – Saifedean Ammous Schulden: Die ersten 5000 Jahre – David Graeber Reinhart & Sbrancia: The Liquidation of Government Debt (Studie, englisch)

VOR-ORT-KURS – 30. MAI 2026

Bitcoin für Einsteiger – digitales Geld für analog Geborene. Kein Trading. Kein Hype. Einfach ein ruhiger, verständlicher Einstieg. https://bitucation.com/kurs/vor-ort-bitcoin

Hinweis: In dieser Folge wird neben meiner Stimme eine KI-generierte Expertenstimme für technische Erklärpassagen eingesetzt. Alle Inhalte werden von mir recherchiert und geprüft.

⚠️ Keine Finanzberatung. Ausschließlich Bildung und persönliche Perspektive.

]]>

item.title: No Second Best - A Bitcoin Podcast – Did the Federal Reserve Just Open the Door to Bitcoin?
item.description:
Jamie Dimon just went on national television to push back against Bitcoin and crypto legislation. Hours later, a Bitcoin exchange gained direct access to the Federal Reserve's settlement rails.

At the same time, Morgan Stanley filed for a spot Bitcoin ETF while geopolitical tensions escalated in the Middle East and Bitcoin surged.

This episode breaks down the growing conflict between the traditional banking system and the emerging Bitcoin financial infrastructure, and why the global monetary system may already be bending toward neutral, hard money.

]]>

item.title: 21Focus - Der Bitcoin-Unternehmer Podcast – Aus der Banken IT in die Welt von Bitcoin - Harald Betz
item.description:
Folge #61 – Ein Gespräch über Banken IT und Bitcoin Bücher

Harald Beetz ist seit fast 20 Jahren selbstständig in der Beratung für Banken und Rechenzentren. Angefangen als Banklehrling bei der Sparkasse, hat er früh den Wandel von Beratung hin zu reinem Vertrieb erlebt und sich auf IT-Projekte spezialisiert. In diesem Gespräch erzählt er, wie er von der Banken IT zu Bitcoin kam, warum er mit seiner Frau einen Roman über Geld schrieb und wie er sich heute zwischen Beratung und Content rund um Bitcoin positioniert.


Du erfährst,

  • wie Harald die Bankenwelt von innen erlebt hat,
  • welche Herausforderungen und Chancen es als IT-Berater gibt,
  • warum er Bitcoin zunächst wieder verkaufte und später mit völlig anderem Blick neu entdeckte,
  • wie aus einer Buchidee seiner Frau ein gemeinsames Projekt wurde
  • und welche unternehmerischen Lektionen er aus fast zwei Jahrzehnten Selbstständigkeit mitnimmt.


Kapitel:

  • 00:00 Begrüßung und Vorstellung
  • 02:15 Vom Banklehrling zum IT-Berater
  • 07:40 Veränderungen in der Bankenwelt seit den 2000er Jahren
  • 15:10 Einstieg in die Selbstständigkeit und erste Projekte
  • 22:05 Gescheiterte Produktidee und der Wechsel zur Beratung
  • 29:15 Erster Kontakt mit Bitcoin und die zweite Begegnung
  • 36:50 Der Roman über Geld und Bitcoin
  • 44:20 Zukunft der Beratung im Banken- und Bitcoin-Umfeld
  • 49:00 Wichtigste Tipps für Vertrieb und Resilienz


Links:

Youtube: youtube.com/@MiesesGeld

Webseite: miesesgeld.de

LinkedIn: linkedin.com/in/haraldbetz


---

21 Focus - Der Bitcoin-Unternehmer Podcast

Hallo, mein Name ist Daniel Wingen und ich suche den Austausch mit anderen Unternehmern die in Bitcoin eine wichtiges Werkzeug für ihr Unternehmen sehen. Ich möchte selbst lernen und andere dazu inspirieren sich mit Bitcoin im eigenen Unternehmen zu beschäftigen.

Hier kannst Du Teil der Community werden und mich kontaktieren:

Telegram Community: ⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠t.me/B21Focus⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠ 

Daniel auf X: ⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠x.com/danielwingen⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠ 

Daniel auf LinkedIn:⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠ linkedin.com/in/danielwingen/⁠

]]>

item.title: Thank God for Bitcoin – To The Unknown Pod Episode 42: Bitcoin and A World At War
item.description:
In this episode, Jordan Bush and Ryan Finlay explore the complex intersections of global conflict, economic warfare, and Bitcoin's role as a resilient alternative in a world at war. They analyze recent Middle East tensions, the decline of the petrodollar, and how Bitcoin offers a form of financial sovereignty amidst geopolitical chaos.

]]>

item.title: VERIFIED by Bitcoin Suisse – Can Bitcoin Escape the Quantum Black Hole?
item.description:
Is quantum computing Bitcoin's biggest hidden risk - and is anyone actually solving it? In this episode, Dominic Weibel and Luca Gnos sit down with Charles Edwards, founder of Capriole Investments, to break down how geopolitics, macro conditions, and quantum risk are shaping Bitcoin's next move. Edwards shares his onchain valuation tools pointing to a ~43% discount from fair value, explains why institutions now dominate Bitcoin's demand structure, and makes the case that quantum risk is already suppressing price - with a potential major repricing on the horizon within 12–24 months.

]]>

item.title: bitcoin ist - Wissen statt FOMO – 17) ... die Trennung von Geld und Privileg
item.description:
Warum geht die Schere zwischen Arm und Reich immer weiter auf, obwohl wir in einer Zeit des globalen Wachstums leben? Es gibt viele Gründe. Aber unser eigenes Geld ist bevorteilt strukturell eine privilegierte Schicht von Menschen!

In dieser Folge tauchen wir ein in den Cantillon-Effekt. Ein Mechanismus, der fast unsichtbar im Hintergrund arbeitet und dafür sorgt, dass neues Geld niemals alle gleichzeitig erreicht. Wer nah an der Quelle sitzt, profitiert; wer weit weg ist, zahlt die Zeche durch steigende Preise.

Was dich in dieser Folge erwartet:

  • Das Champagner-Gleichnis: Wie sich frisches Geld in einer Pyramide verteilt und warum die unteren Gläser oft leer ausgehen.

  • Richard Cantillon: Was ein Goldminenbesitzer aus dem 18. Jahrhundert uns über unser heutiges Fiat-Geldsystem lehren kann.

  • Systemische Benachteiligung: Warum deine Kaufkraft schwindet, noch bevor die Gehaltserhöhung auf dem Konto ankommt.

  • Bitcoin als Antwort: Warum Bitcoin Privilegien abbaut und wie ein System ohne Zentralbank den Cantillon-Effekt fast vollständig eliminiert.

Erfahre, warum Bitcoin weit mehr ist als nur ein neues Asset. Es ist der Versuch, Geld und Privileg zum ersten Mal in der modernen Geschichte voneinander zu trennen.

Du hast Interesse bitcoin zu kaufen, weisst aber nicht genau wie? Hol dir meinen kostenlosen Leitfaden als Download !

🔗 https://tinyurl.com/45rpsjuc

Folge mir auf Instagram/WhatsApp/Telegram für mehr "Wissen statt Fomo" zum Thema Bitcoin.

Alle Links

🔗 www.linktr.ee/bitcoinist

Hat dir die Folge gefallen? Dann abonniere den Podcast und hinterlasse eine Bewertung. Das hilft mir enorm, Wissen über Bitcoin zu verbreiten!

Bleib dran, dein Christian.

]]>
item.itunes:summary:
Warum geht die Schere zwischen Arm und Reich immer weiter auf, obwohl wir in einer Zeit des globalen Wachstums leben? Es gibt viele Gründe. Aber unser eigenes Geld ist bevorteilt strukturell eine privilegierte Schicht von Menschen!

In dieser Folge tauchen wir ein in den Cantillon-Effekt. Ein Mechanismus, der fast unsichtbar im Hintergrund arbeitet und dafür sorgt, dass neues Geld niemals alle gleichzeitig erreicht. Wer nah an der Quelle sitzt, profitiert; wer weit weg ist, zahlt die Zeche durch steigende Preise.

Was dich in dieser Folge erwartet:

  • Das Champagner-Gleichnis: Wie sich frisches Geld in einer Pyramide verteilt und warum die unteren Gläser oft leer ausgehen.

  • Richard Cantillon: Was ein Goldminenbesitzer aus dem 18. Jahrhundert uns über unser heutiges Fiat-Geldsystem lehren kann.

  • Systemische Benachteiligung: Warum deine Kaufkraft schwindet, noch bevor die Gehaltserhöhung auf dem Konto ankommt.

  • Bitcoin als Antwort: Warum Bitcoin Privilegien abbaut und wie ein System ohne Zentralbank den Cantillon-Effekt fast vollständig eliminiert.

Erfahre, warum Bitcoin weit mehr ist als nur ein neues Asset. Es ist der Versuch, Geld und Privileg zum ersten Mal in der modernen Geschichte voneinander zu trennen.

Du hast Interesse bitcoin zu kaufen, weisst aber nicht genau wie? Hol dir meinen kostenlosen Leitfaden als Download !

🔗 https://tinyurl.com/45rpsjuc

Folge mir auf Instagram/WhatsApp/Telegram für mehr "Wissen statt Fomo" zum Thema Bitcoin.

Alle Links

🔗 www.linktr.ee/bitcoinist

Hat dir die Folge gefallen? Dann abonniere den Podcast und hinterlasse eine Bewertung. Das hilft mir enorm, Wissen über Bitcoin zu verbreiten!

Bleib dran, dein Christian.

]]>

item.title: The Bitcoin Treasury Podcast – Saylor's Bitcoin Selling Pivot Has Five Steps — Most People Only Understood One
item.description:
Bitcoin's free float just hit all-time lows. Strive is paying daily dividends. And a brand new yield fund just launched with 4,000 BTC in demand on day one.


Richard Byworth of BYZ Partners covers the put-selling strategy no Bitcoin treasury has executed yet, why daily dividends could trigger Lightning Network adoption, and why the supply vacuum means this can't go on much longer. Alexandre Laizet breaks down Saylor's Bitcoin selling pivot in five steps — and explains why Capital B at 1.3x mNAV is trading below its own bear market average.

item.title: Bitcoin Insights | Bitcoin Podcast mit Philipp 'PJAH' Hartmannsgruber – #16 Im Auto nach El Zonte: Bitcoin in El Salvador mit Robin Seyr und Ben Raich
item.description:
In dieser Episode spricht Philipp mit Robin Seyr und Ben Raich im Auto auf dem Weg von San Salvador nach El Zonte über ihre Eindrücke aus El Salvador. Sie diskutieren die Bitcoin-Adoption, die Adopting Bitcoin-Konferenz, die Sicherheitslage, die alltägliche Nutzung von Bitcoin und die politische Situation unter Präsident Bukele.


Wer ist Robin Seyr?

Robin Seyr ist Host vom täglichen Bitcoin Podcast.


Wer ist Ben Raich?

Ben Raich arbeitet mit Leidenschaft an vielfältigen Projekten im Bitcoin-Space, um Bildung und Adaption von Bitcoin voranzutreiben. Sein Schwerpunkt liegt auf der Produktion hochwertiger Videopodcasts, dem Videoschnitt sowie journalistischen Tätigkeiten.

item.title: Bitcoin.com News Interviews – Is Crypto Near the Bottom? Bitcoin, Hyperliquid, and the Self-Custody Crisis
item.description: <![CDATA[In this episode of Token Narratives, Graham Stone, Alex Richardson, and David Sencil break down a market that still looks shaky on the surface but may be getting closer to a major turning point. They debate whether Bitcoin has more downside ahead, why some cycle indicators still point to a later bottom, and why others are turning more bullish despite ugly macro conditions.The conversation also dives into Hyperliquid: its deal with Circle, whether regulation could eventually crush its edge, and what happens if traditional finance finally moves in on the perpetuals market. They unpack the bigger question of whether Hyperliquid can build a moat before the old system fully wakes up.They also cover one of the darkest themes in crypto right now: the growing physical security risk around self-custody. From bodyguards at conferences to wrench-attack fears and real-world theft tactics, the episode explores whether “not your keys, not your coins” is running into a brutal real-world limit.A wide-ranging conversation on Bitcoin, macro, AI-driven markets, Hyperliquid, security, and the narratives shaping crypto right now.00:50 - Welcome Back and Market Overview02:13 - Crypto Market Analysis03:39 - Glassnode Chart Explanation04:51 - Cycle Length Chart Discussion06:12 - Inflation and Rate Cuts07:43 - Stock Market and Fed Transitions09:10 - Bullish Sentiments and AI Impact12:07 - Crypto and CapEx Spending13:44 - Future of Bitcoin and Crypto23:26 - Hyper Liquid and USDC Partnership27:17 - Regulation and Hyper Liquid's Future33:29 - Hyper Liquid Market Cap Surge33:50 - Crypto Security Concerns41:27 - Conclusion and Wrap-Up]]>
